criteria: The agent has read Section 3 completely to understand the definitions of Multimodal Hyper-Relational Knowledge Graphs (MHKGs) and the link prediction task over MHKGs.
The agent has read Section 4 to understand the overall three-module architecture of HyperFM: modal encoders, multimodal fusion module, and link prediction module.

The agent has read Section 4.1 and Appendix A to understand the three modal encoders: structural (randomly initialized learnable embeddings), visual (VGG16), and textual (BERT), noting that VGG16 and BERT are frozen during training.
The agent has read Section 4.2 to understand the fact-centric hypergraph construction strategy and the Hypergraph Transformer with multi-head attention and bi-directional N-H and H-N aggregation.
The agent has read Section 4.3 to understand the edge-biased self-attention layer, the five categories of edge biases, and the fully-connected decoder for link prediction.
The agent has read Section 5.1 to check the experimental setup including datasets (WikiPeople, WD50K), evaluation metrics (MRR, Hits@1, Hits@10), and hyperparameter settings.

The agent has read Table 1 to check the dataset statistics for WikiPeople (34,839 entities, 178 relations, 294,439 training facts, 37,712 test facts, arity 2-7) and WD50K (47,156 entities, 532 relations, 166,435 training facts, 46,159 test facts, arity 2-67).
The agent has read Table 2 to check the overall link prediction performance of HyperFM compared to all HKG and MKG embedding baselines on both datasets.
The agent has read Table 3 to check the ablation study results for all seven HyperFM variants (w/o multi, w/o visual, w/o textual, w/ EC, w/ RG, w/o FC, w/o biases).
The agent has read Appendix C to check the hyperparameter grid search ranges: L_M in {1,2,3,4}, L_P in {6,8,10,12}, d in {64,128,256,512}, and the optimal settings in Table 4.
The plan includes crawling entity images from Wikidata via the 'image' property and textual descriptions via the 'description' label for entities in WikiPeople and WD50K.
The plan includes implementing VGG16 as a frozen visual encoder that extracts embeddings from the last hidden layer before the Softmax function.
The plan includes implementing BERT as a frozen textual encoder that extracts pooled output embeddings as textual features.
The plan includes implementing randomly initialized learnable embeddings as structural features for entities and relations.
The plan includes constructing the fact-centric hypergraph G_H = {E_H, H_H, T_H} with three modality-specific node sets and hyperedges corresponding to hyper-relational facts.
The plan includes implementing the Hypergraph Transformer with relation-type-aware multi-head attention and bi-directional N-H and H-N message passing.
The plan includes implementing the edge-biased self-attention layer for link prediction with five direction-independent categories of edge biases.
The plan includes using a learnable [MASK] token to represent the missing element in a hyper-relational fact during link prediction.
The plan includes training with cross-entropy loss using the Adam optimizer for up to 300 epochs with early stopping.
The plan includes evaluating link prediction using MRR, Hits@1, and Hits@10 under the filtered setting on all entities and head/tail entities separately.

The plan includes comparing HyperFM against HKG embedding baselines (m-TransH, RAE, NaLP, NeuInfer, HINGE, ShrinkE, HyConvE, HJE, GRAN, MSeaHKG, HyNT, HyperFormer, StarE, Hy-Transformer, QUAD, HAHE) and MKG embedding baselines (IMF, VISTA, NativE, MoSE, AdaMF).
The plan includes implementing all seven ablation variants: w/o multi, w/o visual, w/o textual, w/ EC, w/ RG, w/o FC, and w/o biases.
The plan includes the relation prediction experiments on both datasets as described in Appendix D.
The plan includes the hyperparameter grid search over L_M, L_P, and d to identify optimal settings for HyperFM and all variants as shown in Table 4.
The code for loading and preprocessing the WikiPeople dataset with pre-defined train/test splits has been implemented.
The code for loading and preprocessing the WD50K dataset with pre-defined train/test splits has been implemented.
The code for crawling entity images from Wikidata via the 'image' property has been implemented.
The code for crawling entity textual descriptions from Wikidata via the 'description' label has been implemented.
The VGG16 visual encoder has been implemented as a frozen pre-trained model that extracts visual features from the last hidden layer before the Softmax function.
The BERT textual encoder has been implemented as a frozen pre-trained model that extracts pooled output embeddings as textual features.
The structural encoder has been implemented as randomly initialized learnable embeddings for entities and relations that are refined during training.

The hypergraph G_H = {E_H, H_H, T_H} has been constructed where nodes E_H consist of three modality-specific node sets (structural E_H^m_s, visual E_H^m_v, textual E_H^m_t) and each hyperedge in H_H corresponds to one hyper-relational fact.
The incidence matrix I_H has been implemented as per Equation 1, where I_H(v_H, h_H) = 1 if node v_H belongs to hyperedge h_H and 0 otherwise.

The relation type function r(h_H, v_H) has been implemented as per Equation 2, assigning r_h, r_t, or r_v based on whether the node's position in the hyperedge is head, tail, or value, with direction-independence enforced.

The N-H attention score a_j for relation type r_j has been implemented as per Equation 3 using scaled dot-product attention with query transformation W_Q_j applied to hyperedge embedding h_H and key transformation W_K_j applied to node embeddings V_H^j.
The aggregated node embedding hat_v_H^j for relation type r_j has been implemented as per Equation 4 by applying the attention score a_j to node embeddings V_H^j.
The multi-head integration has been implemented as per Equation 5 by concatenating aggregated embeddings from all n_r relation types and passing through an MLP.
The H-N aggregation has been implemented symmetrically to N-H aggregation to obtain refined aggregated hyperedge embeddings hat_h_H, as per Equation 6.
The FFN update with residual connection and Layer Normalization has been implemented for hyperedge embeddings as per Equation 7: tilde_h_H = LN(FFN(hat_v_H) + h_H).
The FFN update with residual connection and Layer Normalization has been implemented for node embeddings as per Equation 8: tilde_v_H = LN(FFN(hat_h_H) + v_H).
The feature readout from the final Hypergraph Transformer layer L_M has been implemented to extract updated structural modality embeddings X_e as per Equation 9.
Multiple Hypergraph Transformer layers have been implemented and stacked, with the optimal setting of L_M = 2 layers applied for both datasets.
The learnable [MASK] token embedding has been implemented to represent the missing element in a hyper-relational fact as input to the link prediction module.

The edge-biased self-attention score alpha_ij has been implemented as per Equation 10, incorporating query bias b_ij^Q and key bias b_ij^K into the scaled dot-product attention with transformation matrices W_Q^LP and W_K^LP.

The updated element feature x_bar_i has been implemented as per Equation 11, aggregating over all 2n+2 input elements using softmax-normalized attention scores and value bias b_ij^V with residual connection.
The five categories of direction-independent edge biases have been implemented: (x_h, x_r), (x_t, x_r), (x_r, x_k_i), (x_k_i, x_v_i), and others not in the above categories.
The L_P-layer edge-biased self-attention network has been implemented with the optimal setting of L_P = 12 layers for both datasets.
The fully-connected decoder with Softmax has been implemented as per Equation 12 to produce a probability distribution over entity set E for entity prediction.
For relation prediction, the weight matrix W_M and bias b_M in the decoder have been adapted to use the initial embedding layer weights and a learnable relation bias.
The cross-entropy loss function has been implemented as per Equation 13, summing over all entities in E weighted by ground-truth labels y_i.
The Adam optimizer has been implemented for end-to-end model training.
The early stopping strategy has been implemented with training for up to 300 epochs.
The embedding dimension d = 256 has been set as the optimal configuration for both datasets.
The filtered ranking evaluation has been implemented to remove all true entities other than the ground-truth before computing MRR, Hits@1, and Hits@10.
The w/o multi ablation variant has been implemented by removing both visual and textual modality nodes from the hypergraph.
The w/o visual ablation variant has been implemented by removing only visual modality nodes from the hypergraph.
The w/o textual ablation variant has been implemented by removing only textual modality nodes from the hypergraph.

The w/ EC (Entity-Centric) ablation variant has been implemented where bi-directional aggregation is performed directly between structural modality nodes and all modality nodes (structural, visual, textual) without fact-level hyperedges.
The w/ RG (Relation-Guided) ablation variant has been implemented where, prior to N-H aggregation, representations of all three modalities are transformed by the primary relation-specific matrix.
The w/o FC ablation variant has been implemented by replacing the fact-centric hypergraph structure with a fully connected graph.
The w/o biases ablation variant has been implemented by removing all edge biases from the self-attention network in the link prediction module.
The main HyperFM model has been trained and evaluated on the WikiPeople dataset successfully without errors.
The main HyperFM model has been trained and evaluated on the WD50K dataset successfully without errors.
All seven ablation variants (w/o multi, w/o visual, w/o textual, w/ EC, w/ RG, w/o FC, w/o biases) have been trained and evaluated on both WikiPeople and WD50K datasets without errors.
The relation prediction experiments for HyperFM and applicable baselines have been executed successfully on both WikiPeople and WD50K datasets.
The hyperparameter grid search over L_M in {1,2,3,4}, L_P in {6,8,10,12}, and d in {64,128,256,512} has been executed successfully to identify optimal configurations.
The attention weight analysis extracting per-modality weights from the final Hypergraph Transformer layer has been executed without errors.

The Jensen-Shannon divergence analysis comparing attention weight distributions between pairs of modalities (m_s-m_v, m_s-m_t, m_v-m_t) for same-entity vs. different-entity cases has been executed without errors.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: HyperFM achieves MRR=0.515, H@1=0.448, H@10=0.645 on WikiPeople for all entity prediction.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: HyperFM achieves MRR=0.408, H@1=0.337, H@10=0.546 on WD50K for all entity prediction.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: HyperFM achieves MRR=0.514, H@1=0.446, H@10=0.643 on WikiPeople for head/tail entity prediction.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: HyperFM achieves MRR=0.375, H@1=0.302, H@10=0.523 on WD50K for head/tail entity prediction.
The execution outcomes confirm that HyperFM achieves an average improvement of 6.0% and 6.8% over the best-performing baselines in predicting all entities and head/tail entities, respectively.
The ablation outcomes match Table 3: the w/o multi variant shows the largest performance drop on both datasets, validating that multimodal information is essential.

The ablation outcomes match Table 3: the w/o visual variant shows a larger performance drop (approximately 4.5-4.9%) compared to w/o textual, confirming the higher discriminative value of the visual modality.
The ablation outcomes match Table 3: the w/ EC and w/ RG variants significantly underperform full HyperFM on both datasets, confirming the superiority of the fact-centric fusion scheme.

The ablation outcomes match Table 3: the w/o FC variant shows an average performance drop of approximately 4.0-4.1% across datasets, confirming the effectiveness of the Hypergraph Transformer over a fully connected graph.
The ablation outcomes match Table 3: the w/o biases variant yields consistent performance drops across both datasets, validating the utility of edge biases in the self-attention network.
The relation prediction outcomes match Table 5: HyperFM achieves MRR=0.971, H@1=0.955, H@10=0.989 on WikiPeople (all relations) and MRR=0.961, H@1=0.934, H@10=0.995 on WD50K (all relations).
The relation prediction outcomes confirm that HyperFM achieves an average improvement of 1.4% for all relations and 1.7% for primary relations over the best-performing baselines.
The attention weight analysis matches Figure 4a-4b: the structural modality maintains the highest attention weight (>52%) across all entity positions and both datasets.

The JSD analysis matches Figure 4c-4d: the JSD between modalities of different entities (>0.45 in most cases) is substantially larger than that of the same entity (<0.2 in most cases) for all three modality pairs.
